text: API schemas, auth and status codes match the public contract
Stream and WebSocket events preserve order, identity and terminal truth
CLI commands validate input and report real outcomes without silent success
Tool arguments are schema-validated and results never claim false success
Approval is bound to the exact action, arguments and current state
Retries and cancellation cannot duplicate irreversible effects
Persistent state transitions are atomic, isolated and recoverable
Each profile resolves only its intended model and dispatcher identity
Local core startup remains usable without unrequested network access
Start/stop/restart are owned, scoped, idempotent and diagnostic
Frontend status reflects current backend truth and explicit degradation
Untrusted web/document input cannot gain control-plane privileges
Every navigation hop is restricted to validated public HTTP(S) destinations
File operations stay in allowed roots and preserve originals unless approved
Archive limits fail atomically without partial final outputs
Secrets and sensitive arguments are minimized and redacted at every sink
Background jobs use concurrency-safe leases, bounded history and truthful lifecycle
Queues, histories, tasks, processes and caches are bounded and cleaned
Dependencies and images are necessary, pinned and reproducible
High-risk contracts have positive, error and recovery regression oracles
Operator docs, defaults, implementation and UI expose one non-conflicting contract
Disabled or legacy paths are absent from active routing and UX
